title: Daily Briefing — Apr 02
headline: Extreme fear, but bonds and financials aren’t breaking — patience wins today.
body: Right, so we're seeing the market crawl back from the abyss: SPY at 655.24 (+0.8%) but still below the all-important 20 MA (659.27) and 50 MA (675.52). Fear & Greed is buried at 13.9—deep Extreme Fear territory. The VIX sits elevated at 24.72 but is trending down; volatility is easing, not breaking out.

Sector rotation is messy: Energy (XLE) still leads but weakening, while Tech and Discretionary are showing improving momentum from lagging quadrants. Financials (XLF) at 49.44 are approaching their 20 MA (49.22), improving but still in an elevated risk zone. Healthcare continues to lag with XLV below all key MAs.

The contrarian signals are clear: Extreme Fear is historically a window for opportunity (R013), but watch the technicals—most major indices (SPY, QQQ) are still below their daily 20 MA, so no chasing longs here. Bonds (HYG/LQD) are stable, so there’s no macro fire alarm (R001). Abundance mindset—if you missed the move, sit tight and wait for confirmation.
callout_body:
SPY: 659.27 (20 MA) — Needs daily close above for any risk-on
QQQ: 590.17 (20 MA) — Same drill, no long until reclaimed
XLF: 49.22 (20 MA) — Financials must hold for sector health
XLE: 58.67 (20 MA) — Energy rotation at risk, monitor weakness
Fear & Greed < 25 — Historic buy window, but don’t chase
HYG: 79.09 (20 MA) — Bond stability, no equity panic
action_items: Watch for a daily close on SPY or QQQ above their 20 MA for entry signal — no longs until confirmed.
Monitor XLF for support; if financials break down, reduce equity exposure per rule R001.
closing_quote: Patience, react, don’t predict — if it’s in the press, it’s in the price.
